skill.name: deploy
skill.description: Guides deployment setup, analytics integration, and environment health checks for web projects.
skill.summary: Prepare web projects for deployment, analytics, and environment readiness checks.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static weak-crypto, backtick execution, and entropy findings are false positives after reviewing the referenced Markdown files. The remaining risk is medium because this community skill enables Bash plus file editing for deployment, analytics, and health-check workflows. No evidence found of malware, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: Bash and File Editing Permissions in Community Deployment Skill
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash, Write, and Edit for deployment and analytics setup. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can run local commands and change project files.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The tool permissions are explicit in frontmatter and match the skill purpose. Risk depends on user approval of generated commands and edits, so malicious intent is not confirmed.

medium_findings.title: Deployment and Analytics Setup Can Affect Sensitive Configuration
medium_findings.description: The deployment workflow includes environment variable guidance, and the analytics workflow creates tracking configuration. No evidence found of exfiltration, but secrets and tracking identifiers require careful handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files describe configuration work that commonly touches sensitive deployment settings. The content is high level and does not show unsafe secret handling code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static hits occur on frontmatter description metadata. No evidence found of weak cryptographic algorithm use at those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are plain YAML description fields. They contain no hash, cipher, or cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The detected backticks format file names and a sample health-check report. They are Markdown syntax, not shell or Ruby execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is a checklist and output-format example. There is no executable Ruby code or command interpolation.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Static Finding Is a Markdown False Positive
low_findings.description: The file is readable Markdown with frontmatter and Japanese text. No evidence found of binary payloads, encryption, or obfuscated content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows normal text content from the first line onward. The entropy warning appears caused by multilingual Markdown content, not encoded data.

content.user_title: Deploy Sites With Analytics and Health Checks
content.value_statement: Deployment setup often requires hosting configuration, analytics wiring, and readiness checks across several files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through those tasks with focused deployment workflows.
actual_capabilities: Routes deployment, analytics, and health-check requests to focused subdocuments.
Guides Vercel and Netlify deployment setup for web projects.
Describes environment variable setup and preview deployment checks.
Guides Google Analytics or Vercel Analytics integration planning.
Provides a health-check report format for required tools and configuration files.
limitations: Does not deploy a project without user-provided hosting details and credentials.
Does not include complete provider-specific command sequences for every framework.
Requires careful review before running generated Bash commands.
Does not verify production behavior after deployment unless the agent performs checks.

use_cases.title: Launch a Small Web App
use_cases.description: Prepare a project for Vercel or Netlify deployment and identify required configuration files.

use_cases.title: Add Product Analytics
use_cases.description: Plan Google Analytics or Vercel Analytics integration and define basic page and event tracking.

use_cases.title: Check Project Readiness
use_cases.description: Review local tools, workflow files, and configuration before a release or handoff.

prompt_templates.title: Check Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy skill to check whether this project is ready for Vercel or Netlify deployment. Report missing files, tools, and settings.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Hosting Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deploy setup workflow to prepare this web project for deployment. Recommend Vercel or Netlify and explain required configuration changes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Analytics Tracking
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the analytics workflow to add page view and event tracking. Compare Google Analytics and Vercel Analytics for this project.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Release Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use all relevant deploy workflows to create a release plan with hosting setup, analytics, health checks, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Check if my app can be deployed to Vercel.
output_examples.output: A readiness report listing required tools, detected configuration files, missing settings, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Add analytics to this React project.
output_examples.output: A short integration plan that selects an analytics provider, lists configuration changes, and defines key events to track.

output_examples.input: Prepare this project for production release.
output_examples.output: A deployment checklist covering hosting, environment variables, preview deployments, analytics, and post-deploy validation.

best_practices: Review every proposed Bash command before execution.
Keep production secrets in provider environment settings, not client code.
Run a health check before and after changing deployment configuration.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production secrets into prompts or generated files.
Do not enable analytics without confirming privacy and consent requirements.
Do not deploy directly to production before testing preview deployments.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy my project automatically?
faq.answer: It guides deployment setup and validation. Actual deployment depends on user approval, provider access, and project configuration.

faq.question: Which hosting providers does it mention?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on Vercel and Netlify workflows.

faq.question: Can it add Google Analytics?
faq.answer: It can guide Google Analytics setup and tracking planning when the project structure supports it.

faq.question: Can it run commands?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill permits Bash in its metadata, so users should review commands before execution.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use it carefully. Review file edits and avoid sharing secrets in prompts or client-side files.

faq.question: Does it replace a release checklist?
faq.answer: No. It helps create and run checks, but teams should keep their own approval and rollback process.
